text.title: IH Build-Update Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in IH build-update posts. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix keyed to a methodology rule.

antipattern.symptom: Post gets <5 replies despite shipping a "big update"; IH algorithm rate-limits the next 2 posts.
antipattern.root-cause: Body described qualitative wins ("excited about momentum") without numeric anchors.
antipattern.fix: Require 3 numbers per r2. Validator rejects bundles with fewer. Defer the post a week if numbers are not available.

antipattern.symptom: Post body looks fine but replies are 90% "great work!" with no actionable signal.
antipattern.root-cause: "thoughts?" or "any feedback?" invites approval, not specific input.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite the ask to a specific 1-min question (verbatim hook example, A vs B choice, single tactic recommendation). Rule r3.

antipattern.symptom: Reach drops 50% vs prior posts; the post sits below the fold in the IH feed.

root-cause.source: Body length above the sweet spot

antipattern.root-cause: Body > 1500 chars buries the ask and signals low-density narration.
antipattern.fix: Trim body to 600-1200 chars per r4. Move backstory to a comment reply when reader asks. Use one paragraph break per number.

antipattern.symptom: Post receives 20-40% less reach than peer posts the same week.
antipattern.fix: Schedule the post for Tue 8-10am ET per r5. Skip the week rather than post in a dead slot.
